gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A content address, e.g. `blake3:af13ŌĆ”` (64 hex). The kernel hash is **BLAKE3** (fixed in M-103),"] # [doc = " rendered as `blake3:<64-hex>`; this type fixes the shape (`<algo>:<digest>`, matching the"] # [doc = " `provenance.schema.json` pattern) and stays algorithm-agnostic so a future migration is a value"] # [doc = " change, not a type change."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Two validation strengths are offered, deliberately distinct (DN-40 wave-2):"] # [doc = " - [`ContentHash::parse`] / [`ContentHash::from_parts`] ŌĆö **shape-only**, exactly matching the"] # [doc = "   normative JSON-Schema pattern `^[a-z0-9]+:[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$` (which documents that it \"fixes only"] # [doc = "   the shape\"). This is what (de)serialization uses, so the Rust type and the schema agree."] # [doc = " - [`ContentHash::parse_digest`] ŌĆö **algorithm-aware**: additionally requires that a `blake3`"] # [doc = "   address carry a *real* 64-lowercase-hex digest, so a shape-valid-but-bogus stub like"] # [doc = "   `\"blake3:abc\"` is rejected (G2). This is the **centralized** check consumers that hold a"] # [doc = "   genuine, identity-bearing pin (e.g. a manifest dependency hash ŌĆö DN-40 A3) should call, instead"] # [doc = "   of each re-deriving the 64-hex rule. See [`ContentHash::digest_well_formed`]."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq , Hash , PartialOrd , Ord)] pub struct ContentHash (String) ;
gaps.reason: struct `ContentHash` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 ŌĆö every value already copies structurally; DN-128 ┬¦6.1) ŌĆö not emitted as an impl, not a gap

This is the **centralized** check consumers that hold a"] # [doc = "   genuine, identity-bearing pin (e.g. a manifest dependency hash ŌĆö DN-40 A3) should call, instead"] # [doc = "   of each re-deriving the 64-hex rule. gaps.snippet: impl ContentHash { # [doc = " Parse a content address, validating its **shape only**: `algo` is `[a-z0-9]+`, `digest` is"] # [doc = " `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`, separated by a single `:`. This is exactly the normative schema pattern"] # [doc = " (`provenance.schema.json`: `^[a-z0-9]+:[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$`, \"fixes only the shape\"), so the Rust"] # [doc = " type and the on-wire schema agree ŌĆö (de)serialization routes through here. Returns `None` if"] # [doc = " malformed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Note:** this does *not* check that a `blake3` digest is a real 64-hex digest ŌĆö a stub like"] # [doc = " `\"blake3:abc\"` is shape-valid and accepted here. A caller holding an identity-bearing pin"] # [doc = " (not a placeholder) should use [`ContentHash::parse_digest`], which adds the algorithm-aware"] # [doc = " digest check (DN-40 wave-2)."] # [must_use] pub fn parse (s : & str) -> Option < Self > { let (algo , digest) = s . split_once (':') ? ; if algo . is_empty () || digest . is_empty () { return None ; } if ! algo . bytes () . all (| b | b . is_ascii_lowercase () || b . is_ascii_digit ()) { return None ; } if ! digest . bytes () . all (| b | b . is_ascii_alphanumeric () || b == b'_' || b == b'-') { return None ; } Some (ContentHash (s . to_owned ())) } # [doc = " Parse a content address with **algorithm-aware digest validation** (DN-40 wave-2) ŌĆö the"] # [doc = " centralized form. Validates the shape (as [`parse`](Self::parse)) **and**, for the kernel's"] # [doc = " fixed algorithm `blake3` (M-103), that the digest is a *real* digest: exactly"] # [doc = " [`BLAKE3_HEX_LEN`] lowercase hex `[0-9a-f]` (what `blake3::hash().to_hex()` emits). So a"] # [doc = " shape-valid-but-bogus stub like `\"blake3:abc\"` is **rejected**, not silently accepted (G2)."] # [doc = " Unknown algorithms stay **permissive** (shape-only) for forward-compatibility: a future hash"] # [doc = " migration is a value change, not a type change, and the kernel mints only `blake3` today."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This centralizes the check DN-40 A3 first added inline in `mycelium-proj`'s manifest parser,"] # [doc = " so every consumer that holds an identity-bearing pin can share one canonical implementation"] # [doc = " rather than re-deriving the 64-hex rule. Returns `None` if malformed. **It is kept distinct"] # [doc = " from [`parse`](Self::parse)** because the normative JSON Schema is shape-only by design;"] # [doc = " (de)serialization must stay schema-aligned, so the stricter rule is opt-in at the call sites"] # [doc = " that want it."] # [must_use] pub fn parse_digest (s : & str) -> Option < Self > { let h = Self :: parse (s) ? ; if Self :: digest_well_formed (h . algo () , h . digest ()) { Some (h) } else { None } } # [doc = " Is `digest` a well-formed digest for `algo`? For `blake3` (M-103): exactly [`BLAKE3_HEX_LEN`]"] # [doc = " lowercase hex `[0-9a-f]`. For any other (forward-compat) algorithm: permissive ŌĆö the address"] # [doc = " charset already enforced by [`parse`](Self::parse) is sufficient, so this returns `true`."] # [doc = " This is the single source of truth for the algorithm-aware digest rule (DN-40 wave-2)."] # [must_use] pub fn digest_well_formed (algo : & str , digest : & str) -> bool { match algo { "blake3" => { digest . len () == BLAKE3_HEX_LEN && digest . bytes () . all (| b | b . is_ascii_digit () || (b'a' ..= b'f') . contains (& b)) } _ => true , } } # [doc = " Does this address carry a well-formed digest for its own algorithm (DN-40 wave-2)? Convenience"] # [doc = " over [`digest_well_formed`](Self::digest_well_formed) for an already-parsed address ŌĆö lets a"] # [doc = " consumer that received a [`ContentHash`] via the shape-only path (e.g. `Deserialize`) assert"] # [doc = " the algorithm-aware rule without re-parsing."] # [must_use] pub fn has_well_formed_digest (& self) -> bool { Self :: digest_well_formed (self . algo () , self . digest ()) } # [doc = " Build a content address from an algorithm tag and digest, validating the **shape** (`algo` is"] # [doc = " `[a-z0-9]+`, `digest` is `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`). Returns `None` if either part is malformed. This"] # [doc = " is the constructor the content-addressing pass (M-103) uses after computing a digest. Because"] # [doc = " M-103 feeds it a real 64-hex `blake3` digest, the shape check suffices; a caller that wants"] # [doc = " the algorithm-aware guarantee on untrusted parts can compose with"] # [doc = " [`digest_well_formed`](Self::digest_well_formed) or use [`parse_digest`](Self::parse_digest)."] # [must_use] pub fn from_parts (algo : & str , digest : & str) -> Option < Self > { Self :: parse (& format ! ("{algo}:{digest}")) } # [doc = " The algorithm tag (the part before `:`), e.g. `blake3`."] # [must_use] pub fn algo (& self) -> & str { self . 0 . split_once (':') . map_or ("" , | (a , _) | a) } # [doc = " The digest (the part after `:`)."] # [must_use] pub fn digest (& self) -> & str { self . 0 . split_once (':') . map_or ("" , | (_ , d) | d) } # [doc = " The address as a string slice."] # [must_use] pub fn as_str (& self) -> & str { & self . 0 } }
gaps.reason: impl method `parse` body: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

Returns `None` if"] # [doc = " malformed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Note:** this does *not* check that a `blake3` digest is a real 64-hex digest ŌĆö a stub like"] # [doc = " `\"blake3:abc\"` is shape-valid and accepted here.
